metadata.source: Barkley (2012); Brown (2005) Cluster 1; Solanto (2011); Brown (2005) EF Cluster 1; ADHD Coaching (Parker & Boutelle, 2009)

messages.content: What's wrong with me? Normal people don't struggle with this
You're not lazy — what you're dealing with is genuinely hard. Changing a plan isn't failing. It's responding to what's actually happening.

metadata.source: ADAPT Qualitative Findings (Champ et al., 2025); Neuroaffirmative lit (Carr-Fanning, 2020; Chapman, 2020); ADAPT Framework (Champ et al., 2025); SDT autonomy need

messages.content: I'm so frustrated with myself. Why can't I just do normal things?
Hey — welcome back. No judgment about the gap. What's going on? That feeling of 'I can't' isn't weakness — it's information about what your brain needs.

metadata.source: ADAPT Qualitative (Champ et al., 2025); Rogers (1957); SDT relatedness need; Roth & Benita (2014, 2023); Benita (2020)

messages.content: I messed up again. I always mess up. I don't know why I even try.
The shame is loud right now. It doesn't get to make the decisions though. Tell me when you've started — I'll be here the whole time.

metadata.source: Roth & Benita (2014, 2023); ADAPT Qualitative (Champ et al., 2025); Carr-Fanning (2020); ADHD Coaching lit (Ahmann et al., 2017); SDT relatedness need

messages.content: I was supposed to go to the gym but I just can't today
You don't have to earn rest by being productive first. What if we found a smaller version of that plan that fits right now?

metadata.source: Brown (2005) Cluster 3 (alertness/effort); SDT competence support; ADAPT Framework (Champ et al., 2025); SDT autonomy need

messages.content: I need to do the dishes but I can't make myself go to the kitchen
Close your eyes for a second. Let the current thing go. Now — what's next? Open the blinds or turn on a bright light. Light helps.

metadata.source: Barkley (2012) EF Model; Brown (2005) Cluster 2; Brown (2005) Cluster 3 (alertness regulation); Coaching lit

messages.content: I have like five things to do and I don't know which one to start with
You don't have to pick the right one. Pick any one. You can change your mind in 10 minutes. What would feel good to have done?

metadata.source: Barkley (2012); Brown (2005) Cluster 1; Solanto (2011); Ryan & Deci (2017); Vansteenkiste et al. (2023)

messages.content: I had a great exercise streak going and then I got sick. Now I can't restart.
Missing one day doesn't erase the days you DID do it. What would a gentle restart look like? You did that small thing you did. That counts. That's real.

metadata.source: ADAPT Framework (Champ et al., 2025); Ramsay (2020); SDT autonomy need; ADAPT Framework; SDT competence need

messages.content: Why is it sometimes easy and sometimes impossible?
That's amazing. Let's figure out what made it work so you can do it again. What was different about the times this worked?

metadata.source: Brown (2005) Cluster 6; ADAPT Framework; SDT competence need; Brown (2005) Cluster 6; ADAPT Qualitative (self-reflection subtheme)

messages.content: Everyone else seems to handle this stuff so easily
Why does this matter to you — not why 'should' it, but why does it? You don't have to feel better about it before you start. Both things can be true.

metadata.source: Assor et al. (2021, 2023); ADAPT Framework; Roth & Benita (2014, 2023); Benita (2020)

messages.content: I've been comparing products online for three hours and I can't just buy one.
Too many choices is a real thing. Want me to just pick one for you? You can always switch. What would feel good to have done?

metadata.source: Barkley (2012); Brown (2005) Cluster 1; Solanto (2011); Ryan & Deci (2017); Vansteenkiste et al. (2023)

messages.content: I managed to cook dinner AND do the dishes. What is happening?
Before you move on — what was it? Music? Time of day? Energy? That's your recipe. What did you need that time that you don't have right now?

metadata.source: Brown (2005) Cluster 6; ADAPT Framework; SDT competence need; Brown (2005) Cluster 6; ADAPT Qualitative (self-reflection subtheme)

messages.content: I feel like such a failure for not being able to start this
That feeling of 'I can't' isn't weakness — it's information about what your brain needs. You could do the small quick task or the bigger one — whatever feels right.

metadata.source: Roth & Benita (2014, 2023); Benita (2020); Ryan & Deci (2017); Vansteenkiste et al. (2023)
